title: Patch the Invoice IDOR
objective: Patch the invoice endpoint so users can only access their own records.
briefing: Access logs show one account (user 1042) requesting invoice ids in sequence, viewing records that aren't theirs. The endpoint needs an ownership check, not just a login check.
hints: Restarting the service won't add a missing check that was never coded.
The fix has to happen in the invoice endpoint's code itself.
debrief: The fix adds a server-side check on every request: does invoice.owner_id match the logged-in user's id? If not, return 403 regardless of whether the id exists. This closes the IDOR by enforcing object-level authorization instead of relying on users only ever guessing their own ids. General rule for Broken Access Control (A01:2021): authorization checks belong on the server, per-object, on every request - never inferred from the UI only showing 'your' links.

logs: [ACCESS] invoice 1043 viewed by user 1042 - no ownership check performed

patch /var/www/api/invoices.php --enable.output:
patched /var/www/api/invoices.php
applying ownership check middleware... done

logs_append: [FIX] ownership check enabled for /api/invoices - cross-user access now returns 403
